zh.lifeSummary: 當Hubert de Vaux於1102年出生在英國波藍德易爾頓時，他的父親Robert de Vaux32歲，母親Beatrice de Munchensy17歲。他於大約1120年與Mrs Hubert Vaux在法國諾門迪結婚。他去世於1167年之後在英國波藍德易爾頓，埋葬在英國波藍德易爾頓。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Vaux
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of various places in northern France called Vaux, from the Old French plural of val ‘valley’. Compare English Vause and French Devaux .

History: Richard Vaux (1749–90) emigrated from Sussex, England, to Philadelphia, PA, in early youth. Calvert Vaux, landscape architect, emigrated from London, England, to America in 1850 and designed several major US parks, including Central Park in New York City (with Frederick Olmstead). — This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors (along with its variant Veaux and altered form Devoe ) and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America.

variants.name: Vause
meanings: of Norman origin, a habitational name from Vaux (see Vaux ), a common French placename, singular and plural of val ‘valley’, the plural form being Latinized in surnames as de Vallibus. Norman lords with this surname are recorded in 12th- and early 13th-century records in Cumberland (Irthington), Devon (Farwood, in Colyton), East Yorkshire (Warter), Essex (Belchamp), and Leicestershire (Belvoir). Compare Vale .

nickname perhaps from Middle English fals, faus, vaus (Old French fals, faus, Old English fals) ‘deceitful, disloyal, dishonest, mendacious’, but it is more likely the name arises from 1, with the common Anglo-Norman French substitution of le for de in their surname.

nameMeanings.name: Hubert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Old French name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from hug ‘heart, mind, spirit’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. It was popular among the Normans, who introduced it to Britain, where it was later reinforced by settlers from the Low Countries. An 8th-century St Hubert succeeded St Lambert as bishop of Maastricht and is regarded as the patron of hunters, since, like St Eustace , he is supposed to have seen a vision of Christ crucified between the antlers of a stag.

variants.name: Hupprecht
meanings: Old French name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from hug ‘heart, mind, spirit’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. It was popular among the Normans, who introduced it to Britain, where it was later reinforced by settlers from the Low Countries. An 8th-century St Hubert succeeded St Lambert as bishop of Maastricht and is regarded as the patron of hunters, since, like St Eustace , he is supposed to have seen a vision of Christ crucified between the antlers of a stag.

An 8th-century St Hubert succeeded St Lambert as bishop of Maastricht and is regarded as the patron of hunters, since, like St Eustace , he is supposed to have seen a vision of Christ crucified between the antlers of a stag.

It was popular among the Normans, who introduced it to Britain, where it was later reinforced by settlers from the Low Countries. An 8th-century St Hubert succeeded St Lambert as bishop of Maastricht and is regarded as the patron of hunters, since, like St Eustace , he is supposed to have seen a vision of Christ crucified between the antlers of a stag.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: When Hubert de Vaux was born in 1102, in Irthington, Cumberland, England, his father, Robert de Vaux, was 32 and his mother, Beatrice de Munchensy, was 17. He married Mrs Hubert Vaux about 1120, in Normandy, France. He died after 1167, in Irthington, Cumberland, England, and was buried in Irthington, Cumberland, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Hubert de Vaux est né en 1102, à Irthington, Cumberland, Angleterre, son père, Robert de Vaux, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Beatrice de Munchensy, avait 17 ans. Il a épousé Mrs Hubert Vaux environ 1120, à Normandie, France. Il est décédé après 1167, à Irthington, Cumberland, Angleterre, et a été enterré à Irthington, Cumberland, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Hubert de Vaux 1102 in Irthington, Cumberland, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Robert de Vaux 32 und seine Mutter Beatrice de Munchensy 17. Er hat um 1120 in Normandie, Frankreich Mrs Hubert Vaux geheiratet. Er starb nach 1167 in Irthington, Cumberland, England und wurde in Irthington, Cumberland, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Hubert de Vaux è nato il nel 1102 nel Irthington, Cumberland, England, il suo padre, Robert de Vaux, aveva 32 anni e il suo madre, Beatrice de Munchensy, aveva 17 anni. Lui sposato Mrs Hubert Vaux circa 1120, nel Normandie, Francia. Morì dopo il 1167, nel Irthington, Cumberland, England, e fu sepolto a Irthington, Cumberland, England.

ko.lifeSummary: Hubert de Vaux(이)가 1102년 영국 c벌랜드 이르스잉턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert de Vaux(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Beatrice de Munchensy(은)는 17세였다. 그는 대략 1120년 프랑스 노먼디에서 Mrs Hubert Vaux(와)과 결혼했다. 그는 1167년 이후 영국 c벌랜드 이르스잉턴에서 사망했으며 영국 c벌랜드 이르스잉턴에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Hubert de Vaux nasceu em 1102, em Irthington, Cumberland, Inglaterra, seu pai, Robert de Vaux, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Beatrice de Munchensy, tinha 17 anos. Ele casou-se com Mrs Hubert Vaux aproximadamente 1120, em Normandie, França. Ele faleceu após 1167, em Irthington, Cumberland, Inglaterra, e foi sepultado em Irthington, Cumberland, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Hubert de Vaux родился в 1102 г. в местности иртингтон, кумбирланд, Англия, его отцу по имени Robert de Vaux было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Beatrice de Munchensy, 17. Он женился на женщине по имени Mrs Hubert Vaux около 1120 г. в местности норманди, Франция. Он умер после 1167 г. в местности иртингтон, кумбирланд, Англия и был похоронен в местности иртингтон, кумбирланд, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Hubert de Vaux nació en 1102, en Irthington, Cumberland, Inglaterra, su papá, Robert de Vaux, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Beatrice de Munchensy, tenía 17 años. Se casó con Mrs Hubert Vaux aproximadamente 1120, en Normandie, Francia. Murió después de 1167, en Irthington, Cumberland, Inglaterra, y fue sepultado en Irthington, Cumberland, Inglaterra.
